commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Telegram draft-stream dispatch to queue and rotate answer blocks by assistant-message index, preserves reply-payload metadata through delivery hooks, adds a public SDK helper for that metadata, and expands regression coverage plus the SDK API baseline.

PR surface: Source +245, Tests +786, Generated 0. Total +1031 across 14 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for source-level reproduction: current main keeps non-final Telegram blocks as mutable previews, so later blocks or final text can overwrite them. I did not establish a live Telegram run in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human PR follow-up is needed because the branch still has a stale-edit message-loss defect, needs real Telegram proof, and adds a public SDK helper that should be maintainer-approved.

target.title: Preserve Telegram streamed text blocks between tool calls
target.body:
Fixes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/87326.

## Summary

- Preserve Telegram streamed answer blocks across tool-call / assistant-message boundaries instead of letting later blocks edit the same draft preview.
- Keep chunks from the same assistant message in one Telegram draft, and rotate only when the next answer block belongs to a different assistant message or after tool-progress-only drafts.
- Preserve reply-payload assistant-message metadata through `beforeDeliver`, TTS cleanup, reply-payload-sending hooks, and message-sending hooks so Telegram can match queued blocks after delivery rewrites.
- Expire queued Telegram block rotations when delivery is cancelled, skipped, rewritten to no answer text, or normalized away, without deleting still-pending earlier rotations.

## User-facing bug

When Telegram block streaming was enabled, answer text emitted between tool calls could be visible briefly and then disappear. A later assistant block or the final answer could edit the same Telegram draft preview, so users lost intermediate findings such as "Site A shows X" after the agent checked another tool.

## Exact repro

The focused regression drives Telegram dispatch through the same shape as the issue:

1. Stream a partial answer for assistant message 0.
2. Queue the final block for assistant message 0.
3. Cross an assistant-message/tool boundary.
4. Deliver assistant message 0's block.
5. Stream assistant message 1.

Before the fix, the message-1 partial could overwrite the message-0 draft. After the fix, the message-0 block is delivered first, then Telegram rotates to a fresh answer draft for message 1.

```bash
OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts
```

## Fix summary

Telegram dispatch now tracks queued answer blocks by assistant-message index. The queue is filled from `onBlockReplyQueued`, matched after final `beforeDeliver` / TTS / message-sending rewrites, and drained when a queued block is cancelled, skipped, or no longer contains answer text. Assistant-message-start no longer rotates immediately when there are queued prior blocks; it delays rotation until those blocks settle so the old message is finalized before the next assistant message writes to a fresh draft.

To make that reliable, reply-payload metadata is preserved across the auto-reply delivery hooks that may replace payload objects. The public plugin SDK exposes only a narrow `getReplyPayloadAssistantMessageIndex()` helper, not the full internal metadata bag.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Telegram answer text emitted between tool calls remains visible after the next assistant block starts. The first assistant block is delivered on the existing draft, then Telegram rotates to a fresh draft before writing the next assistant message.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout on Node v24.4.1, branch `fix/telegram-preserve-stream-blocks`, commit `83ad501f009629d0719b36f62bffe3017e2501f9`, running the Telegram extension dispatch path with a local Telegram API/draft-stream harness because no live Telegram token/chat is configured in this workspace.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: from `/Users/alex/PR/projects/openclaw__openclaw/worktrees/88682`, run `OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts`; the regression invokes `dispatchTelegramMessage`, streams `Site A partial`, queues `Site A final`, crosses `onAssistantMessageStart`, delivers the queued prior block, then streams `Site B partial`.

Evidence after fix: terminal capture from the local Telegram dispatch proof shows the prior assistant block is written before the rotation, and the next assistant message writes after `forceNewMessage()`:

```text
draft update #1: Site A partial
draft update #2: Site A final
forceNewMessage()
draft update #3: Site B partial
deliverReplies: not called for the streamed answer path
```

Observed result after fix: the retained Telegram draft contains `Site A final` before the boundary rotation, so the later `Site B partial` no longer overwrites the previous visible answer. The same local run also exercises skipped/cancelled/non-answer queued block cases so stale queued rotations do not block the next answer draft.

What was not tested: live Telegram bot roundtrip with real credentials was not run; this workspace has no Telegram token/chat configured.

## Supplemental validation

Commands run after the fix:

```bash
git diff --check origin/main
```

```bash
/Users/alex/PR/projects/openclaw__openclaw/repo/node_modules/oxfmt/bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256 extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts extensions/telegram/src/draft-stream.test-helpers.ts extensions/telegram/src/lane-delivery-text-deliverer.ts extensions/telegram/src/lane-delivery.test.ts src/auto-reply/dispatch.test.ts src/auto-reply/dispatch.ts src/auto-reply/reply/before-deliver.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/reply-dispatcher.ts src/auto-reply/reply/reply-payload-sending-hook.ts src/plugin-sdk/reply-payload.ts
```

```bash
./node_modules/.bin/oxlint extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts extensions/telegram/src/lane-delivery-text-deliverer.ts extensions/telegram/src/lane-delivery.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/draft-stream.test-helpers.ts src/plugin-sdk/reply-payload.ts src/auto-reply/dispatch.ts src/auto-reply/dispatch.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/before-deliver.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/reply-dispatcher.ts src/auto-reply/reply/reply-payload-sending-hook.ts
```

```bash
node --max-old-space-size=8192 --import tsx scripts/generate-plugin-sdk-api-baseline.ts --check
```

```bash
CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo
```

```bash
CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.non-agents.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test-non-agents.tsbuildinfo
```

```bash
OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts
```

```bash
OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/lane-delivery.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/draft-stream.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/reply-payload.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-index.test.ts
```

```bash
OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/before-deliver.test.ts src/auto-reply/dispatch.test.ts
```

```bash
OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts -t "forwards payload metadata into onBlockReplyQueued context"
```

Supplemental results:

- `bot-message-dispatch.test.ts`: 112 passed.
- Telegram / draft / plugin SDK / plugin contract target set: 185 Telegram tests, 53 plugin SDK tests, and 4 plugin contract tests passed.
- auto-reply target set: 5 `before-deliver` tests and 17 dispatch tests passed.
- `dispatch-from-config` metadata regression: 1 passed, 188 skipped by filter.
- `tsgo` passed for `test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json` and `test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.non-agents.json`.
- `plugin-sdk-api-baseline` check passed.
- `git diff --check`, `oxfmt --check`, and `oxlint` passed.

## Known gaps

- `codex review --base origin/main` was run twice before updating this PR. It did not complete a final verdict because the review tool attempted local commands that hit the shared heavy-check lock, unsupported Vitest `--runInBand`, and `.vite-temp` EPERM in this worktree sandbox. It emitted no actionable P1/P2 finding before I terminated the hung review process.

## AI-assisted disclosure

This change was implemented with AI assistance.
